metadata.title: Build a Fourier Square Wave with Claude Code + Manim
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL = Fourier partial sums; CRIMSON = Gibbs overshoot annotation. INK for all text.
metadata.note: Medhavy register. B00=brand intro; B01-B04=Fourier square simulation; B05=brand outro.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown — Claude Code, Manim, and the physics, taken apart.

The prompt. Claude Code terminal: claude — write a Manim scene, Fourier series for a square wave of period L. Sum the first nine odd harmonics — terms n equals one, three, five, seven, nine — each with coefficient four over n-pi. Show the partial sum building up. Mark the Gibbs overshoot at the discontinuity. Three things: the rule, the numbers, what to render.

props.command:
claude "write a Manim scene:
  Fourier series: f(x) = Σ(4/nπ)sin(nπx/L)
  sum odd n=1,3,5,7,9; L=1
  animate each term adding in
  mark Gibbs 9% overshoot at edge"

beats.narration_text: The CLI writes the file. Before running — read it. Find this line: four divided by n-pi times sign of n-pi-x over L. That coefficient is the entire physics of Fourier series for a square wave. Check: at x equal L over two, the series converges to one point zero, because sign of n-pi over two alternates plus and minus one and the sum is the Leibniz formula for pi over four. Thirty seconds. Never skip.

props.code:
# fourier_square.py  —  Claude Code output
import numpy as np

L = 1.0
x = np.linspace(0, L, 500)

# Partial sum — odd harmonics only
def partial_sum(x, N_terms):
    result = np.zeros_like(x)
    for k in range(N_terms):  # k=0,1,2,...
        n = 2*k + 1           # n=1,3,5,...
        # ← the physics: Fourier coefficient
        result += (4 / (n * np.pi)) * np.sin(n * np.pi * x / L)
    return result

# At x=L/2: converges to 1.0 (Leibniz series)

beats.narration_text: Run it. Two things to verify. First: the Gibbs overshoot. At the rising edge, the N-equals-nine curve climbs to about one-point-zero-nine — nine percent above the true value of one. That overshoot does not shrink as you add more terms. It is a fundamental property of Fourier series at a jump discontinuity. Second: at x equals L over two, the curve converges exactly to one point zero. Both checks pass.

Iterate: claude — update fourier-square dot py — increase N to ninety-nine terms. Show the overshoot is still nine percent. Show the square wave is sharper everywhere else. The Gibbs phenomenon refuses to go away. One parameter, one verification, one re-run. The loop is the skill.

props.command:
claude "update fourier_square.py:
  → increase N_terms from 5 to 50
  → overlay N=5 and N=50 curves
  → show Gibbs overshoot persists at 9%"

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist and Educational A-I. On YouTube, at Nik Bear Brown. Nik Bear Brown dot com.
